title: Fresh Australian Cauliflower Paella
description: A mustardy, light one‑pot paella with cauliflower, white wine and mother‑sauce base, perfect for a Buddhist‑temple‑friendly meal.
instructions: Heat the paella pan over medium heat and drizzle in the olive oil.
Add the diced onion and garlic, cooking until the onion becomes translucent.
Stir in the diced red bell pepper and cauliflower florets, sautéing for about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle in the paprika and saffron, mixing well to coat the vegetables.
Pour in the white wine and let it reduce for 2 minutes until the liquid has almost evaporated.
Add the vegetable stock, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, and season with salt and pepper.
Reduce the heat to low, cover loosely, and let the paella cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
After cooking, stir in the Dijon mustard, adjust seasoning, and serve hot.
